title: brickellia scoparia var. subauriculata
intro: Brickellia scoparia var. subauriculata, also known as Mexican Brickellia or False Yerba Santa, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Brickellia scoparia var. subauriculata can be prepared into teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting healing.
